title: phaseolus vulgaris var. carneoflavescens
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. carneoflavescens, also known as the common bean or kidney bean, is a variety of the common bean plant (Phaseolus vulgaris) that is native to Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce the risk of certain diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The health benefits of Phaseolus vulgaris var. carneoflavescens include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, regulate blood sugar levels, and provide essential nutrients such as protein, fiber, and minerals. Herbal preparations of Phaseolus vulgaris var. carneoflavescens include dried bean extracts, capsules, and teas, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
